title: Other Public Health Agency of Canada publications: HPCDP, Vol 42, No 1, January 2022 - Canada.ca
content:
## Other PHAC publications
- Information for authors
Previous | Table of Contents
https://doi.org/10.24095/hpcdp.42.1.07
Researchers from the Public Health Agency of  Canada also contribute to work published in other journals. Look for the  following articles published in 2021:
Généreux M, David MD, O’Sullivan T, […] Michel P ,  et al. Communication  strategies and media discourses in the age of COVID-19: an urgent need for  action. Health Promot Int. 2021;36(4):1178-85. https://doi.org/10.1093/heapro/daaa136
Lagacé M, Doucet A, Dangoisse P, Bergeron CD . The “vulnerability” discourse in times of COVID-19:  between abandonment and protection of Canadian francophone older adults. Front  Public Health. 2021;9:662231. https://doi.org/10.3389/fpubh.2021.662231
Liu S , Dzakpasu S , Nelson C , et  al. Pregnancy outcomes during the COVID-19 pandemic in Canada, March to August  2020. J Obstet Gynaecol Can. 2021. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jogc.2021.06.014
Sultan  S, Siedler MR, Morgan RL, Ogunremi T ,  et al. An international needs assessment survey of guideline developers demonstrates  variability in resources and challenges to collaboration between organizations.  J Gen Intern Med. 2021. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11606-021-07112-w
Weeks  M , Park  SB , Ghanem  S , Plebon-Huff  S , Robert  AM ,  MacKay H, LeBlanc  A .  A systematic review of the prevalence of posttraumatic stress disorder reported  in Canadian studies. In: Ricciardelli R, Bornstein S, Hall A, Carleton RN,  editors. Handbook of Posttraumatic  Stress: Psychosocial, Cultural, and Biological Perspectives. 1st ed. New York (NY): Routledge; 2021:38-108. https://doi.org/10.4324/9781351134637
